commands.review_summary:
The PR parallelizes bundled MCP catalog loading, bundled LSP server startup, and top-level MCP/LSP initialization in embedded agent attempts, with post-hoc LSP tool deduplication.

PR surface: Source +117. Total +117 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source for the latency shape: current main serializes MCP materialization before LSP startup, and the MCP catalog loop awaits each configured server in order. I did not run a live slow-server before/after trace in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer review of failure semantics and startup fan-out, not an autonomous repair lane.

target.title: perf(agents): parallelize MCP and LSP initialization to reduce cold-start latency
target.body:
## Summary

Every agent request incurs ~6-7 seconds of latency before the model starts generating. The bottleneck is the `bundle-tools` phase which loads all MCP servers and LSP tools upfront. The root cause: MCP server connections, LSP server spawning, and their coordination are all **sequential**.

## Root Cause

1. **MCP connections sequential**: `getCatalog()` iterates servers one at a time with a `for` loop, connecting and listing tools for each (~1-2s per server, 4 servers = 4-8s)
2. **LSP spawning sequential**: `createBundleLspToolRuntime()` spawns and initializes LSP servers one at a time
3. **MCP and LSP sequential**: `attempt.ts` awaits MCP completion before starting LSP

Trace evidence (from production, before fix):
```
[trace:embedded-run] prep stages: totalMs=5889 stages=bundle-tools:5792ms@5809ms
[trace:embedded-run] prep stages: totalMs=6020 stages=bundle-tools:5898ms@5915ms
[trace:embedded-run] prep stages: totalMs=6527 stages=bundle-tools:6435ms@6449ms
[trace:embedded-run] prep stages: totalMs=7207 stages=bundle-tools:7132ms@7207ms
```

In all cases, `bundle-tools` accounts for **96-99% of total prep time**.

## Fix

### 1. Parallelize MCP server connections (`agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts`)

`getCatalog()` is restructured into 3 phases:
- **Phase 1 (sync)**: Resolve all transports and pre-compute safe names
- **Phase 2 (parallel)**: Connect to all servers and list tools concurrently via `Promise.all`
- **Phase 3 (sync)**: Assemble results

Cold-start wall-clock time is now bounded by the **slowest server** instead of the **sum of all servers**.

### 2. Parallelize LSP server initialization (`agent-bundle-lsp-runtime.ts`)

`createBundleLspToolRuntime()` uses the same 3-phase pattern:
- **Phase 1 (sync)**: Validate all configs
- **Phase 2 (parallel)**: Spawn and initialize all LSP servers via `Promise.allSettled`
- **Phase 3 (sync)**: Assemble results, deduplicate tool names

### 3. Run MCP and LSP in parallel (`attempt.ts`)

Instead of:
```ts
bundleMcpRuntime = await getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime(...);
bundleMcpRuntime = await materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun(...);
bundleLspRuntime = await createBundleLspToolRuntime(...);
```

Now uses:
```ts
const [mcpResult, lspResult] = await Promise.all([
  mcpInitTask?.catch(err => { log.warn(...); return undefined; }),
  lspInitTask?.catch(err => { log.warn(...); return undefined; }),
]);
```

Each task catches its own errors so one failure does not block the other.

### 4. Preserve cross-runtime tool-name deduplication (`attempt.ts`)

When MCP and LSP initialize in parallel, LSP construction no longer sees materialized MCP tool names. A post-hoc dedup step filters LSP tools whose names collide with MCP tools after both complete, preserving the pre-existing priority (MCP names win, duplicate LSP tools are skipped with a warning log).

## Expected Improvement

For a typical setup with 4 MCP servers each taking ~1-2s to connect and list tools:
- **Before**: 6-8s (4 servers × ~1.5s + LSP time, sequential)
- **After**: ~1.5-2s (max server time + LSP time in parallel)

The `bundle-tools` prep stage should drop from dominating 96-99% of prep time to a much smaller fraction.

## Verification

All 158 tests pass across the modified surfaces:

| Test file | Result |
|----------|--------|
| `agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts` | 33/33 ✅ |
| `agent-bundle-lsp-runtime.test.ts` | 3/3 ✅ |
| `attempt.test.ts` | 122/122 ✅ |

TypeScript (`tsgo`), lint (`oxlint`), and formatting (`oxfmt`) all pass.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: The `bundle-tools` prep stage takes 6-7s on every agent request, accounting for 96-99% of total prep time, because MCP server connections (`connect()` + `tools/list`) and LSP server spawning run sequentially. Simple text-only requests pay the full tool-init cost even when no tools are called.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw main @ f91350485c33, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` test runner.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts` — 33 MCP runtime tests (covers catalog loading, tool materialization, transport lifecycle, backoff, idle sweep, lease management)
  2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-lsp-runtime.test.ts` — 3 LSP runtime tests
  3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.test.ts` — 122 embedded attempt runner tests
  4. `pnpm tsgo` — full-project typecheck (passes)
  5. `oxfmt` — formatting check on all changed files (passes)
- **Evidence after fix**:
  - All 158 tests pass with zero regressions.
  - Code review confirms the sequential `for` loop in `getCatalog()` was replaced with `Promise.all(serverPlans.map(async ...))`. Each server connects and lists tools independently; the slowest server determines total wall-clock time.
  - Code review confirms the sequential MCP-then-LSP await chain in `attempt.ts` was replaced with `Promise.all([mcpTask, lspTask])`.
  - Cross-runtime tool-name dedup was preserved: after parallel init, LSP tools that collide with MCP names are filtered with a warning log (same behavior as before, just deferred from construction time to post-init).
- **Observed result after fix**:
  - MCP server connections now fire concurrently. With N servers each taking T ms, wall-clock time drops from `N × T` to `max(T)`. For a 4-server setup at ~1-2s each, this means ~1.5-2s instead of ~6-8s.
  - MCP and LSP initialization no longer block each other; they run in the same `Promise.all`.
  - Bundle MCP runtime caching (session-scoped, idle-TTL-gated) is unchanged — warm-request tool preparation remains near-instant.
- **What was not tested**:
  - Live multi-MCP-server end-to-end trace with real networked MCP servers (e.g., Notion, Z.ai, Web Reader) was not captured in this environment because spawning external MCP server processes in this CI/container context is unreliable.
  - The deterministic parallel-timing proof test (`"connects to multiple MCP servers in parallel"`) was attempted but removed because the mock-stdio MCP server harness requires real `node:child_process` spawning that does not complete reliably under this environment's resource constraints.
  - Recommendation: after merge, a maintainer or contributor with a real slow-MCP setup should capture `[trace:embedded-run] prep stages:` logs before and after to confirm the ~4-5s reduction in `bundle-tools` wall time.

## Files Changed

- `src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts` — Parallelize MCP catalog connections
- `src/agents/agent-bundle-lsp-runtime.ts` — Parallelize LSP server initialization
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — Parallelize MCP+LSP coordination + preserve cross-runtime dedup

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
